statement: Spring Boot 4 uses Jackson 3 (tools.jackson.*). GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer was removed; use GenericJacksonJsonRedisSerializer with tools.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper instead.
solution: 1) Replace import: org.springframework.data.redis.serializer.GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer → GenericJacksonJsonRedisSerializer. 2) Inject ObjectMapper: Add tools.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper as a parameter to your CacheManager @Bean method (Spring Boot 4 auto-configures it). 3) Update serializer usage: Change fromSerializer(new GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer()) to fromSerializer(new GenericJacksonJsonRedisSerializer(jacksonObjectMapper)). 4) The ObjectMapper must be from tools.jackson.databind (Jackson 3), not com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.

statement: RedisCacheConfiguration.getTtl() was removed in Spring Data Redis 4.x. Code using it as a fallback (e.g. in createCacheConfig) will not compile.
solution: Replace redisCacheConfiguration.getTtl() with an explicit Duration. Example: .orElse(redisCacheConfiguration.getTtl()) → .orElse(Duration.ofMinutes(15)). Use the same default TTL you had configured (e.g. spring.cache.redis.time-to-live) or a sensible default like 15 minutes. The getTtl() method no longer exists on RedisCacheConfiguration.

statement: Shedlock 7.x changed LockConfiguration constructor from (String taskName, Instant lockAtMostUntil, Instant lockAtLeastUntil) to (Instant lockAtMostUntil, String taskName, Duration lockAtMostFor, Duration lockAtLeastFor).
solution: Replace: new LockConfiguration(taskName, Instant.now().plusSeconds(maxHold), Instant.now().plusSeconds(minHold)) with new LockConfiguration(Instant.now(), taskName, Duration.ofSeconds(maxHold), Duration.ofSeconds(minHold)). Note: 1) First param is now lockAtMostUntil (Instant), 2) Second is taskName (String), 3) Third and fourth are Duration, not Instant. Add import java.time.Duration.

statement: HttpHeaders.containsKey(String) was removed in Spring Framework 6.x / Spring Boot 4. Use containsHeader(String) instead.
solution: Replace headers.containsKey(headerName) with headers.containsHeader(headerName). Same semantics: returns true if the header is present. Typically found in ClientHttpRequestInterceptor implementations when checking if a header should be propagated (e.g. X-Forwarded-Host, X-Forwarded-Proto).

statement: spring-boot-webmvc-test pulls in spring-cloud-contract-wiremock which may conflict with Spring Boot 4. Tests using @EnableWireMock need wiremock-spring-boot instead.
solution: 1) Add: testImplementation 'org.wiremock.integrations:wiremock-spring-boot:4.2.0'. 2) Exclude contract wiremock: testImplementation('org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-webmvc-test') { exclude group: 'org.springframework.cloud', module: 'spring-cloud-contract-wiremock' }. 3) Keep @EnableWireMock and @ConfigureWireMock from org.wiremock.spring. 4) Do not use @AutoConfigureWireMock or other spring-cloud-contract-wiremock annotations. WireMock client APIs (stubFor, post, aResponse) and wiremock.server.port config remain unchanged.

statement: NoResourceFoundException constructor changed from 2 params (HttpMethod, resourcePath) to 3 params (HttpMethod, requestUri, resourcePath) in Spring Framework 7 / Spring Boot 4. Tests constructing it for exception handlers will not compile. getMessage() format also changed.
solution: **Before:** `new NoResourceFoundException(HttpMethod.GET, "/path")` and `assertEquals("No static resource /path.", error.getDetails().get(0))`. **After:** `new NoResourceFoundException(HttpMethod.GET, "/path", "/path")` — pass requestUri as second param (can match resourcePath for tests). For assertions, use `assertTrue(error.getDetails().get(0).contains("/path"))` instead of exact match since getMessage() format changed. **Why:** Spring Framework 7 added requestUri to separate original request URI from resource path; message format is no longer stable.

statement: ResponseEntity.getStatusCodeValue() was removed in Spring Framework 6. Code using it (e.g. in servlet filters setting response status) will not compile.
solution: **Before:** `response.setStatus(responseEntity.getStatusCodeValue())`. **After:** `response.setStatus(responseEntity.getStatusCode().value())`. **Why:** getStatusCodeValue() was deprecated in Spring 5.3 and removed in Spring 6; getStatusCode() now returns HttpStatusCode with .value().

statement: HttpHeaders.addIfAbsent(String, String) was removed in Spring Framework 7 / Spring Boot 4 because HttpHeaders no longer implements MultiValueMap. Code using it (e.g. in ClientHttpRequestInterceptor for header propagation) will not compile.

HttpHeaders.get(headerName) was removed in Spring Framework 7 / Spring Boot 4 because HttpHeaders no longer implements MultiValueMap. Code using headers.get(key).getFirst() or headers.get(key).get(0) will not compile.
solution:
**Before:** `Objects.requireNonNull(headers.get(HttpHeaders.CONTENT_TYPE)).getFirst()` or `headers.get(key).get(0)`

**After:** `headers.getFirst(headerName)`

**Why:** HttpHeaders no longer implements MultiValueMap; get() is gone. Use getFirst(headerName) for the first value of a header.

statement: Spring Cloud Contract 5 removed @AutoConfigureWireMock. Tests using spring-cloud-contract-wiremock must migrate to wiremock-spring-boot-standalone with @EnableWireMock and @InjectWireMock.
solution:
**Before:** Dependency `spring-cloud-contract-wiremock`, annotation `@AutoConfigureWireMock(port = 0)`, injection `@Autowired WireMockServer`.

**After:** Add `org.wiremock.integrations:wiremock-spring-boot-standalone:4.0.9`, use `@EnableWireMock` (class-level), use `@InjectWireMock WireMockServer` (field injection). WireMock client APIs (stubFor, get, aResponse) remain unchanged.

**Why:** Spring Cloud Contract 5 removed the WireMock integration; wiremock-spring-boot-standalone provides the replacement.

statement: Spring Boot 4 uses Spring Data 4.x, which requires Redisson 4.x. Redisson 3.52.x is incompatible with Spring Data Redis 4.x.
solution: Bump redisson-spring-boot-starter from 3.52.x to 4.2.0 or later. Exclude redisson-hibernate-53 and redisson-hibernate-6 if not using Hibernate L2 cache.

statement: The compile configuration is deprecated in Gradle. Using compile.exclude module causes deprecation warnings.
solution: Replace compile.exclude with implementation.exclude in the configurations block.

statement: The mainClassName property on the Application plugin is deprecated in favor of mainClass.set(...).
solution: Replace mainClassName = "com.example.Application" with mainClass.set("com.example.Application").

statement: Spring Boot 4 uses Apache Kafka 4.x. Tests depending on kafka-server-common, kafka-metadata, or kafka-streams-test-utils at 3.9.x may fail due to version mismatch with spring-kafka-test.
solution: Align Kafka test dependencies with Spring Boot 4: use kafka-server-common, kafka-metadata, and kafka-streams-test-utils at 4.2.0 (or BOM-provided version).

statement: Spring Boot 4 aligns with JUnit Jupiter 6.x. Projects on JUnit 5.10.x or 5.14.x may need to upgrade to junit-jupiter:6.0.3 for full compatibility.
solution: Bump org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter and junit-jupiter-params to 6.0.3.

statement: Bump paysafe-op-dependencies BOM to at least 4.0.2-RC1 for Spring Boot 4.
Bump paysafe-op-commons-framework to at least 6.0.0-RC1.
Bump paysafe-op-commons-test to at least 5.0.0-RC1.
Bump paysafe-ss-logging to at least 11.0.0-RC1.
Bump paysafe-op-flowable to at least 2.0.0-RC1.
Bump paysafe-op-http-interfaces-spring-boot-starter to at least 4.0.0-RC1.
Bump paysafe-wallet-usage-kafka-producer-spring3 to at least 3.0.0-RC1.

Spring Boot 4 + Gradle integrationTest + wiremock-spring-boot: stubs in src/test/resources are not on integrationTest classpath by default; WireMock 3 needs mappings/ and __files/ under filesUnderClasspath; bodyFileName fails with Not found in blob store if __files are missing. wiremock-spring-boot JUnit extension defaults resetWireMockServer=true and clears classpath stubs between @ParameterizedTest invocations.
solution:
**Before:** Stubs lived as `src/test/resources/component-test-mappings/<scenario>/*.json` and shared bodies in `src/test/resources/__files/**`. `integrationTest` only packaged `src/integrationTest/resources`, so `@EnableWireMock(filesUnderClasspath = "component-test-mappings/…")` saw no files (log: no stub mappings). After copying resources, flat `*.json` still did not load under WireMock 3; responses using `bodyFileName` failed with `Not found in blob store: platform/….json`. `@ParameterizedTest` then failed on later rows because stubs were reset between invocations.

**After:** (1) `testImplementation("org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-webmvc-test") { exclude group: "org.springframework.cloud", module: "spring-cloud-contract-wiremock" }` plus `testImplementation "org.wiremock.integrations:wiremock-spring-boot:4.2.0"`. (2) Extend `processIntegrationTestResources` (type `Copy`): copy `component-test-mappings/**/*.json` and `api-test-mappings/**/*.json` from `src/test/resources` and use `eachFile` to insert a `mappings` path segment before the filename (e.g. `…/registrations-activation/mappings/foo.json`). (3) For each direct child dir under `component-test-mappings` and `api-test-mappings`, copy all of `src/test/resources/__files` into `<scenario>/__files` so `bodyFileName` resolves. (4) On every `@ConfigureWireMock` that uses `filesUnderClasspath`, set `resetWireMockServer = false` if the class uses `@ParameterizedTest` or needs stubs to survive across invocations. Keep `duplicatesStrategy = DuplicatesStrategy.INCLUDE` if the task merges with other resource copies.

**Why:** Integration test classpath is separate from `test` resources; WireMock 3’s file source expects `mappings/` + `__files/` siblings under the classpath root passed to `filesUnderClasspath`; `bodyFileName` is resolved against `__files`. The wiremock-spring JUnit extension resets servers before each test method when `resetWireMockServer` is true (default), which removes stubs loaded from the classpath at context startup—harmful for parameterized methods that reuse the same Spring context.

statement: After Spring Boot 4 + paysafe-op-commons-framework 6.x, MockMvc tests using @WithUserDetails on secured servlet APIs can return 401: OneplatformJwtSecurityFilter only accepts a Jwt principal, not UserDetails from @WithUserDetails.
solution:
**Before:** Component tests annotated methods with `@WithUserDetails("123456")` and called `mockMvc.perform(MockMvcRequestBuilders.post("/onboarding/v1/...").contentType(...).content(...))` expecting 200. Application security uses OAuth2 resource server + Paysafe filters (`OneplatformJwtSecurityFilter`, `OneplatformAuthenticationFilter`) from `paysafe-op-commons-framework`.

**After:** Remove `@WithUserDetails` for those requests and add Spring Security Test’s JWT post-processor, building a JWT whose `sub` is the customer id (and other claims as needed), e.g. `mockMvc.perform(MockMvcRequestBuilders.post(...).with(jwt().jwt(j -> j.subject(customerId).claim("email", "user@example.com"))).header(...).content(...))`. Optionally centralise in a small `RequestPostProcessor customerJwt(String id)` helper.

**Why:** `OneplatformJwtSecurityFilter` validates the `SecurityContext` authentication for paths it handles; if the principal is not a `Jwt`, it throws `UnauthorizedAccessException` (HTTP 401). `@WithUserDetails` loads `UserDetails` directly and never creates a `Jwt`. `jwt()` supplies a `Jwt` so the same filter chain can run and `OneplatformAuthenticationFilter` can convert claims into `OneplatformUser` (e.g. `sub` for `getId()`), matching how real requests are authenticated.

statement: Angular 19 introduces a new signal-based input binding API to enhance component input performance and clarity.
solution: To migrate, replace traditional @Input() bindings with signal inputs by using the schematic command: `ng generate @angular/core:signal-input-migration`.

statement: The `zone.js` dependency is now optional in Angular 19 for improved performance with zoneless change detection.
solution: Remove `zone.js` from your dependencies and update your bootstrap process to include `provideZonelessChangeDetection()`.

statement: Angular 19 deprecates `ngOnChanges` in favor of signal-based lifecycle hooks for component change detection.
solution: Replace `ngOnChanges` implementations with the new signal-based mechanism by updating your components to use the new lifecycle API.

statement: Updates to `angular.json` are required to support the new build system improvements in Angular 19.
solution: Update `angular.json` to include new build options by following the migration guide provided in the official Angular documentation.

statement: The RxJS version is updated in Angular 19, requiring developers to ensure compatibility with the new RxJS APIs.
solution: Update your project to use the latest RxJS version and refactor code to accommodate any changes in the RxJS API.

statement: Angular 20 introduces a breaking change in the way signals are handled, requiring migration to the new signal API.
solution: Replace previous signal handling patterns with the new API as demonstrated in the blog's code examples.

statement: The deprecated ReactiveFormsModule has been replaced by a new module, requiring developers to update their imports.
solution: Replace all instances of `ReactiveFormsModule` with `NewReactiveFormsModule` in your application imports.

statement: Angular 20 introduces a new schematic command for migrating input signals to the latest format.
solution: Run the schematic command `ng generate @angular/core:signal-input-migration` to auto-migrate input signals.

statement: Updates to `angular.json` require enabling strict mode for the build configuration to comply with Angular 20 standards.
solution: In `angular.json`, set `projects.*.architect.build.options.strict` to `true`.

statement: The dependency on `zone.js` has been reduced, allowing for more efficient change detection mechanisms in Angular 20.
solution: Update your application to remove unnecessary `zone.js` dependencies as per the guidelines provided in the blog.
